name: node-signal-exit
title: node-signal-exit
summary: Fire an event no matter how a process exits
description:
Fire an event no matter how a process exits
When you want to fire an event no matter how a process exits:
reaching the end of execution.
explicitly having process.exit(code) called.
having process.kill(pid, sig) called.
receiving a fatal signal from outside the process
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-signal-exit because Fire an event no matter how a process exits.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-signal-exit as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-signal-exit with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-signal-exit --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
